old_claim.note: Codex (Early Book Form) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The codex book form is the earlier bound-manuscript format that medieval monastic scriptoria copied, preserved, and illuminated.

source_shape.source_locator: The article defines the codex as a folded book form in pre-print manuscript culture and treats its transition from ancient scroll technology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies codex as the bound book form of manuscript culture that precedes medieval scriptoria.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Scriptoria copied and preserved manuscripts; the corrected edge keeps codex as historical book-form lineage, not as a religious or agricultural cause of monasticism.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: codex_early_book_form remains a historical_predecessor edge with review-level source support.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to monastic community organization without manuscript copying.
A stronger source showed medieval scriptoria primarily copied scrolls rather than codices.
A separate manuscript_culture node replaced codex_early_book_form as the better predecessor.
short_reason: Codex is the manuscript book-form lineage for scriptoria.
